exemplar.title:
How does the Simulated Annealing Algorithm compare to the Tabu search algorithm when
analysing their time complexities and solution quality/accuracy when solving the Knapsack
Problem?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does the Simulated Annealing Algorithm compare to the Tabu search algorithm when
analysing their time complexities and solution quality/accuracy when solving the Knapsack
Problem?

strengths: Title and introduction communicate focus and purpose clearly
Research question is explicit, focused and consistently anchored throughout
Methodology is replicable with well-outlined variables, procedures and code excerpts
weaknesses: Minor lapses in phrasing and transitions affect fluidity
Justification for parameter choices and dataset size is thin
Specific dataset details (e.g. item counts) are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed Topic Communication and Research Question firmly in the top band (5â6), with Methodology in the 3â4 band at level 4. Overall, the investigation is communicated accurately and maintains a clear, appropriate focus, but minor articulation and methodological justification gaps mean the work sits at the lower end of the top band. A score of 5 reflects strong performance without exemplary expression or fully robust rationale.

strengths: Technical terminology is used accurately and consistently
Mathematical formulation and LaTeX rendering of the knapsack model are clear and precise
Derivation of the SA acceptance probability demonstrates strong conceptual grasp
weaknesses: Subject knowledge treatment is good but stops short of deep theoretical analysis (e.g. convergence proofs)
Heavy reliance on informal web sources weakens academic rigor
Some bibliographic entries (e.g. MetropolisâHastings) are incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While subject knowledge is solid (band 3â4) and terminology usage meets the upper band (5â6), the overall understanding is closer to excellent than merely adequate. Correct and consistent use of advanced terms, clear mathematical exposition and strong conceptual clarity justify elevating the criterion to 5, despite minor theoretical and citation shortcomings.

strengths: Research application remains consistently relevant to the question and links literature to experiment
Data analysis is clearly presented in tables and graphs with meaningful interpretations
Discussion forms a coherent argument about trade-offs between SA and TS, with structured evaluation of strengths and limitations
weaknesses: No statistical testing of results (e.g. variability, significance)
Alternative algorithmic strategies and parameter schedules are not critically compared
Evaluation depth is moderate rather than highly critical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows clear relevance (band 7â9) and good analytical support (band 4â6), with a structured, critical discussion (band 4â6). Taken as a whole, the work demonstrates strong critical thinking within the 7â9 range; the minor omissions in statistical rigor and broader evaluation support a score near the top of that band, at 9.

strengths: Logical overall structure with a clear table of contents and numbered sections
Consistent pagination, headings and a coherent bibliography style
weaknesses: Several figures are low resolution with inconsistent captions
Long code blocks disrupt flow and reduce readability
Some layout inconsistencies in indentation and spacing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure fully supports reader navigation (band 3â4), but layout weaknessesâlow-quality images, inconsistent captions and code formatting issuesâprevent a top score. A score of 3 reflects a presentation that aids evaluation yet contains noticeable lapses.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student outlines variables, controlled factors, hardware, and step-by-step procedures, and includes code excerpts.  Source use is adequate but heavily weighted toward informal web pages, YouTube videos and GitHub, showing only partial evidence of informed selection.  While the method is replicable, justification of parameter choices and dataset size is thin, and the range of secondary literature on the algorithms is modest.  These qualities align with a âmostly completeâ methodology, sitting comfortably in the 3-4 band; the fuller coverage merits the upper value of 4.

Data are processed into tables, averages and graphs, with corresponding explanations of what the numbers imply for time and accuracy.  The analysis is relevant and mostly supported, but statistical treatment (variability, significance) is absent, and some claims rest on qualitative reasoning.  This matches the middle band (4-6); completeness warrants a midpoint score of 5.

The essay follows a logical structure: title page, contents, numbered sections, subsections, tables, evaluation and bibliography.  Each element supports reader navigation.  Minor issues (occasional figure placement, some long code blocks disrupting flow) prevent a perfect presentation, but the structure clearly aids understanding, justifying the top band with a score of 4.

The student discusses strengths, limitations and sources of error in a structured evaluation table, demonstrating some critical perspective.  The argument linking evidence to the conclusion (SA trades time for accuracy, TS the reverse) is coherent.  However, evaluation depth is moderate; alternative methodologies or broader implications are not rigorously explored.  This aligns with the 4-6 descriptor; a 5 reflects competent but not exceptional critical evaluation.

Technical termsâmetaheuristic, stochastic, deterministic, tabu tenure, cooling schedule, time complexityâare used correctly and consistently.  Mathematical symbols are properly formatted, and big-O notation is applied accurately.  Minor slips ("vviotal", "iters") are typographical rather than conceptual.  Overall usage demonstrates strong command, meeting the upper-band descriptor; a score of 5 is assigned to reflect small inconsistencies.

All research (algorithm descriptions, big-O theory, experimental data) is clearly directed toward answering the stated question.  The implementation, trials and metric definitions show a coherent link between literature and investigation.  Nonetheless, the study is confined to a single dataset size and borrowed code, limiting the depth of application.  This places the work solidly in the 7-9 band but nearer the lower edge; 7 is awarded.

Pagination, table formatting, headings and MLA-styled bibliography are present.  Some images are low resolution; figure captions are inconsistent, and code screenshots are difficult to read.  Overall layout supports evaluation but with noticeable weaknesses, placing it in the 3-4 band; a 3 is awarded to reflect these lapses.

The essay explains NP-hardness, big-O notation, SA cooling schedules and TS tabu lists with correct mathematical expressions and code fragments, demonstrating sound subject knowledge.  However, deeper theoretical treatment (e.g., probabilistic convergence of SA, memory structures of TS) is only sketched, and citations are occasionally superficial.  The knowledge is therefore "good" rather than "excellent," fitting the 3-4 band; the consistent relevance of most content justifies a 4.

The research question is explicitly worded and appears verbatim on the title page and in the introduction.  It is sharply focused (two named algorithms, two metrics, one problem instance) and remains the anchor for data collection, analysis and conclusion sections.  Occasional digressions (general NP-hard commentary) are brief, so connection to discussion is consistently maintained.  This matches the top-band descriptor; a score of 5 (rather than 6) reflects that the question could have been tightened further by specifying dataset characteristics or implementation language.

The student presents an unambiguous title, a clearlyâstated research question and a succinct introduction that frames the knapsack problem, the two meta-heuristics, and the dual performance metrics to be studied.  The purpose (comparing SA and TS with respect to time and accuracy) is reiterated in several places, and the focus remains consistent throughout the paper.  Minor lapses in phrasing (e.g., âanalysing their time complexitiesâ) do not detract from overall clarity.  Hence the work fulfils the descriptor of being âcommunicated accurately and effectively with clear and appropriate purpose and focus,â meriting a score in the 5-6 band; 5 is awarded because expression, while clear, is not exemplary.

annotations.comment: Reliance on informal web sources weakens academic rigor; incorporate peer-reviewed literature to strengthen evidence base.
Mathematical formulation of the KSP is presented clearly with correct objective and constraint equations.
The title clearly communicates the investigationâs focus by naming the problem and the two algorithms under study, establishing precise context for the reader.
Research question is explicit and focused, but could be sharpened by specifying dataset characteristics or implementation language to further guide analysis.
Word count is provided at the start and falls within the 4000-word limit, fulfilling the formatting requirement.
The table of contents accurately maps all sections and subsections, providing clear navigation for the reader.
Indentation of nested entries in the contents could be improved for enhanced readability and visual hierarchy.
Consider adding visible page numbers in the footer to support cross-referencing and ease of navigation.
Introduction provides a concise overview of the Knapsack Problem and its NP-hard classification, demonstrating solid subject knowledge.
Define âcombinatorial optimizationâ when first introduced to support readers unfamiliar with the term.
The distinction between stochastic SA and deterministic TS is clearly articulated, showing accurate use of technical terms.
Methodology description omits specific dataset size details; specify item counts to clarify experimental scope.
Include a brief rationale for choosing Python and specified hardware to justify methodological decisions.
Citation style for background sources is inconsistent; apply uniform formatting to MLA references throughout.
Add a small numeric example for n and W to illustrate the KSP formulation concretely.
Objective and constraint equations are rendered accurately in LaTeX, enhancing precision and clarity.
Transition into the SA algorithm section lacks a linking sentence explaining its relevance to solving the KSP.
Justify parameter selections (initial temperature, iteration counts) within the SA description to strengthen methodological rationale.
Derivation of the acceptance probability $$P=\exp(-\Delta f / T)$$ is detailed and demonstrates strong subject understanding.
Reference to the MetropolisâHastings criterion lacks a full bibliographic citation; add complete source details.
Discussing alternative cooling schedules (e.g., linear, logarithmic) would add critical evaluation of SA parameters.

rubricData.name: Computer Science EE
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: The Computer Science Extended Essay is a 4,000-word independent research project that allows students to explore a specific topic within computer science. This essay emphasizes technical analysis and original thought, encouraging students to delve into areas such as algorithm efficiency, software development, or the impact of computing technologies. By engaging in the EE, students apply their theoretical knowledge to practical scenarios, enhancing their problem-solving and analytical skills, while contributing original insights to the field of computer science.
